title: Repository Baseline, Node 24, TypeScript 7, Compatibility, and Reproduction · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A public science-learning team incrementally builds reproducible strict TypeScript Pokedex repository from original fixed PokéAPI payloads while retaining prior TypeScript, HTTP, Git, repository-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reproducible strict TypeScript Pokedex repository while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Inspect revision, worktree, package metadata, lockfile, configuration inheritance, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor.
Record supported Node 24 release, module system, platform, built-in fetch and readline surfaces, signal behavior, and package-manager contract.
Use TypeScript 7 CLI deliberately while retaining the documented TypeScript 6 compatibility package where programmatic tooling still requires it.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 7938 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile Node, native TypeScript, compatibility, clean install, emitted runtime, and package evidence; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
